summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined several specific developments in small UAS activity. Ukraine unveiled the Gnome-NS, a new ground-based "mother drone" designed to carry FPVs, and also presented the Gnom-ND wheeled-tracked FPV carrier drone. A Russian fiber-optic FPV drone reached Kharkiv for the first time since the start of the full-scale invasion, striking the city and prompting comments from Syniehubov. In Zaporizhzhia, Ukrainian forces used FPV drones to knock out a Russian Kasta-2E2 radar, while in Orikhiv, a Russian FPV drone strike injured a 67-year-old civilian. Ukraine’s I-SEE system was highlighted for tracking Russian drones in real time without emitting radio signals, and Motor-G was reported to be shipping 200,000 drone motors per month, making it Ukraine’s largest engine manufacturer.
